title: Source Operationalization Decision
description: The operationalization classification produced for every source before any pipeline advancement. One of: reject, note_only, capability_candidate, architecture_experiment, training_lab_only, fine_tune_later.

source_id.description: Identifier of the source being classified.

source_type.description: Source kind: paper, repo, blog, issue, alert, user_failure, note_candidate, etc.

decision.description: The operationalization decision for this source.

classification.description: Human-readable classification label summarizing the decision.

rationale.description: Why this decision was made. Required for audit and operator review.

actionability_score.description: How actionable this source is at the system/workflow layer. 0 = purely theoretical, 100 = immediately callable/deployable.

community_signal_score.description: Community interest or adoption signal. 0 = no signal, 100 = widely adopted/starred.

hermes_relevance_score.description: How relevant this source is to Hermes's current capability gaps and mission.

requires_model_training.description: True if the source requires model architecture changes, pretraining, fine-tuning, or tokenizer redesign beyond local system-layer changes.

requires_external_service.description: True if the source requires a paid external API, cloud service, or non-local dependency.

recommended_lane.description: The recommended downstream lane: discovery (hold/review), runtime (capability pipeline), architecture (experiment pipeline), or external (Obsidian/other).

next_artifact.description: Suggested next artifact path or action in the recommended lane.

kill_criteria.description: Required for architecture_experiment decisions. Defines when the experiment should be killed.

note_target.description: Optional target vault or note path for note_only decisions.
